texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: VA-Oncology Prostate Cancer Template User Guide
texts.text: VA-Oncology Prostate Cancer Template User Guide

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Background Information
texts.text: Background Information

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Prostate cancer is a commonly diagnosed malignancy in VHA. Recent approval of several new treatment options has increased complexity of cancer care. A more efficient mechanism for capturing data to monitor cancer care quality in the VHA is essential. Currently, the large amount of free text information in the medical record makes it difficult to retrieve necessary data to assess the quality of cancer care in a prospective and continuous manner. Reminder dialogs allow data to be entered and extracted in a standardized manner that will enable us to more efficiently monitor quality of cancer care.
texts.text: Prostate cancer is a commonly diagnosed malignancy in VHA. Recent approval of several new treatment options has increased complexity of cancer care. A more efficient mechanism for capturing data to monitor cancer care quality in the VHA is essential. Currently, the large amount of free text information in the medical record makes it difficult to retrieve necessary data to assess the quality of cancer care in a prospective and continuous manner. Reminder dialogs allow data to be entered and extracted in a standardized manner that will enable us to more efficiently monitor quality of cancer care.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1 General Information
texts.text: 1 General Information

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of this guide is to provide the end-user with directions on how to perform medical documentation using the VA-Oncology Prostate Template. The VA-Oncology Prostate Template was developed and is supported by the National Oncology Program Office in Specialty Care Services (part of Patient Care Services). REDACTED
texts.text: The purpose of this guide is to provide the end-user with directions on how to perform medical documentation using the VA-Oncology Prostate Template. The VA-Oncology Prostate Template was developed and is supported by the National Oncology Program Office in Specialty Care Services (part of Patient Care Services). REDACTED

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Recommended Users
texts.text: Recommended Users

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The primary users of the Prostate Cancer Template are oncology providers. Secondary uses are advance practice oncology providers, urologists, and cancer registrars.
texts.text: The primary users of the Prostate Cancer Template are oncology providers. Secondary uses are advance practice oncology providers, urologists, and cancer registrars.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2 Installation
texts.text: 2 Installation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The facility Clinical Applications Coordinator (CAC)/Health Information Specialist (HIS) at each site has received instructions on how to load and implement the VA-Oncology Prostate Template. These instructions are part of the PXRM_2_0_38_IG which accompanies this patch. The primary intended use of the VA-Oncology Prostate Template is as a template that is pulled into an open progress note rather than as a standalone note. The VA-Oncology Prostate Template should be available in a shared folder in the template drawer in CPRS. The location and naming convention for this shared folder will vary by site. Users should contact their local help desk or local CAC/HIS if the VA-Oncology Prostate Template cannot be located in CPRS.
texts.text: The facility Clinical Applications Coordinator (CAC)/Health Information Specialist (HIS) at each site has received instructions on how to load and implement the VA-Oncology Prostate Template. These instructions are part of the PXRM_2_0_38_IG which accompanies this patch. The primary intended use of the VA-Oncology Prostate Template is as a template that is pulled into an open progress note rather than as a standalone note. The VA-Oncology Prostate Template should be available in a shared folder in the template drawer in CPRS. The location and naming convention for this shared folder will vary by site. Users should contact their local help desk or local CAC/HIS if the VA-Oncology Prostate Template cannot be located in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Facilities might choose to link the template to a specific standalone note title rather than use it as a template that is inserted into an open progress note. However, doing so might limit the availability of the template to the range of providers who could make use of the template. The title of the standalone progress note is VA-ONCOLOGY PROSTATE.
texts.text: Facilities might choose to link the template to a specific standalone note title rather than use it as a template that is inserted into an open progress note. However, doing so might limit the availability of the template to the range of providers who could make use of the template. The title of the standalone progress note is VA-ONCOLOGY PROSTATE.

texts.orig: 3 Usage

texts.label: text
texts.orig: provider can complete those sections of the template that are relevant to a particular patient, or that have been selected by a facility (for example based on the quality indicators of interest to a facility). However, once a section is begun, all subsections must either be completed or the check removed from the top level of that section. If a standard of care is knowingly not being followed please document the reason in the template.

texts.orig: 4 Documentation:
texts.text: 4 Documentation:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The VA-Oncology Prostate Template is separated into three sections diagnosis/work-up, treatment and post-treatment.
texts.text: The VA-Oncology Prostate Template is separated into three sections diagnosis/work-up, treatment and post-treatment.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5 Definitions for Each Section Diagnosis Section

texts.label: text
texts.orig: □ Laboratory Tests
texts.text: □ Laboratory Tests

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Prostate Specific-Antigen (PSA) - the last 10 (if available) PSA lab results performed at the local VA site will display in the template. User has option of pulling these lab results into progress note by clicking on appropriate box. Additional/outside lab results can be entered by clicking on corresponding box. Indicate level of test results and date of test.
texts.text: Prostate Specific-Antigen (PSA) - the last 10 (if available) PSA lab results performed at the local VA site will display in the template. User has option of pulling these lab results into progress note by clicking on appropriate box. Additional/outside lab results can be entered by clicking on corresponding box. Indicate level of test results and date of test.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Testosterone – the last 10 (if available) Testosterone lab results performed at the local VA site will display in the template. User has option of pulling these lab results into progress note by clicking on appropriate box. Additional/outside lab results can be entered by clicking on corresponding box. Indicate level of test results and date of test.

User has option of pulling these lab results into progress note by clicking on appropriate box. Additional/outside lab results can be entered by clicking on corresponding box. texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 13 Risk Status
texts.text: 13 Risk Status

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Indicate high, low, or intermediate risk status.
texts.text: Indicate high, low, or intermediate risk status.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: High = (Stage >=T2c) AND (Gleason >=8) AND (PSA >20)
texts.text: High = (Stage >=T2c) AND (Gleason >=8) AND (PSA >20)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Intermediate = (Stage T2b) OR (Gleason =7) OR (PSA >10 AND PSA <20) Low = (Stage T1c/T2a) AND (Gleason <=6) AND (PSA<= 10ng/ml)
texts.text: Intermediate = (Stage T2b) OR (Gleason =7) OR (PSA >10 AND PSA <20) Low = (Stage T1c/T2a) AND (Gleason <=6) AND (PSA<= 10ng/ml)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: □ ECOG Performance Status
texts.text: □ ECOG Performance Status

texts.label: text
texts.orig: ECOG Scale definitions:
texts.text: ECOG Scale definitions:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 0 = Fully active, able to carry on all pre-disease performance without restriction
texts.text: 0 = Fully active, able to carry on all pre-disease performance without restriction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 1 = Restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out work of a light or sedentary nature, e.g., light house work, office work
texts.text: 1 = Restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out work of a light or sedentary nature, e.g., light house work, office work

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 2 = Ambulatory and capable of all self-care but unable to carry out any work activities. Up and about more than 50% of waking hours
texts.text: 2 = Ambulatory and capable of all self-care but unable to carry out any work activities. Up and about more than 50% of waking hours

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 3 = Capable of only limited self-care, confined to bed or chair more than 50% of waking
texts.text: 3 = Capable of only limited self-care, confined to bed or chair more than 50% of waking

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 4 = Completely disabled. Cannot carry on any self-care. Totally confined to bed or chair 5 = Dead
texts.text: 4 = Completely disabled. Cannot carry on any self-care. Totally confined to bed or chair 5 = Dead

texts.orig: 26 Initial Entries
texts.text: 26 Initial Entries

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Users should document only in sections that are pertinent to their patient. There are no required fields at this point. However, once a section is opened, users are required to answer all questions within that section, or uncheck the box for that section (in which case no information from that section will be included).
texts.text: Users should document only in sections that are pertinent to their patient. There are no required fields at this point. However, once a section is opened, users are required to answer all questions within that section, or uncheck the box for that section (in which case no information from that section will be included).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: a. Missing Required Fields:
texts.text: a. Missing Required Fields:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Whenever a section is opened the user is required to answer all questions in that section. If the user clicks on finish without answering all questions in a section a pop-up box will appear directing the user back to the specific unanswered question. For example, the ECOG section is not a required entry unless the user clicks on the checkbox for that section. Once that section is opened the user is required to select a score from the list. If the user clicks on finish without selecting an ECOG score the user will get the following error message:
